title: Count occurances of a value

posts.author: rphilip (Philip Roberts)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have two boards with a 2-way connection. Both are for tracking weekly events and data about those events.

  • There will always be an item in both for each week.
  • The item names in both boards are the date of the Saturday each week (yyyy-mm-dd) format.
  • The item in board B for a given date will always link to an item in board A with a different date.

Board A is the “primary” board.

Board B is “secondary”, and has a “Connect Boards” column that selects an item from Board A and several mirror columns displaying data from board A.

  • Only around half of the items in board A are selected to end up in board B. This selection is manual.
  • As only about half of the items from board A are selected, most of the selected items from board A will end up being connected to multiple items in board B.

I would like to display the number of times an Item from board A is selected to reoccurs in board B. That count can be shown on either board A or board B. Is there a way to do this in monday.com?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Use a formula column & replace Test 2 with the name of the connected column

{Test 2#Count}

image585×253 9.8 KB

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: rphilip (Philip Roberts)
posts.cookedHtml: Should I do this in board A or board B?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: If I’m fully understanding your question, it should be in Board A

posts.author: rphilip (Philip Roberts)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks, that worked exactly how I want it to.
